### finding

- Template: `assuming that the storage tank contained <num> mathrm ft <num> of salt rather than <num> mathrm ft <num> used before we correct the earlier result <num> - <num> <num> mathrm ft <num> mathrm kg <num> <num> mathrm ft <num> mathrm kg and conclude that the drain tank contained <num> mathrm kg of carrier salt at the beginning of <num> mathrm u power operations.`
- Entities: component:drain tank
- ornl-4449: ORNL-4449
  - values: (3.0, 3.0, 1.13, 3.0, 4708.0, 182.6, 3.0, 3.0, 113.0, 1.13, 3.0, 4638.0, 233.0)
  - Assuming that the storage tank contained $3.0\mathrm{ft}^3$ of salt rather than $1.13\mathrm{ft}^3$ used before, we correct the earlier result $4708 - 182.6(3.0\mathrm{ft}^3)\mathrm{kg} + 113.0(1.13\mathrm{ft}^3)\mathrm{kg}$ and conclude that the drain tank contained $4638\mathrm{kg}$ of carrier salt at the beginning of $^{233}\mathrm{U}$ power operations.
- ornl-4658: ORNL-4658
  - values: (30.0, 3.0, 113.0, 3.0, 4707.0, 1826.0, 30.0, 3.0, 1130.0, 113.0, 3.0, 4638.0, 233.0)
  - Assuming that the storage tank contained $30\mathrm{ft}^3$ of salt rather than $113\mathrm{ft}^3$ used before, we correct the earlier result $4707 - 1826(30\mathrm{ft}^3)\mathrm{kg} + 1130(113\mathrm{ft}^3)\mathrm{kg}$ and conclude that the drain tank contained $4638\mathrm{kg}$ of carrier salt at the beginning of $^{233}\mathrm{U}$ power operations.

### measurement

- Template: `in fact the grade cgb graphite obtained <num> years ago for the msre satisfies this requirement. <num> however the graphite structure must be much more restrictive to prevent gaseous fission products particularly <num> mathrm xe from diffusing into the graphite.`
- Units: years
- Entities: alloy-material:graphite, reactor:MSRE
- ornl-tm-2511: ![](images/4ded454a32824359092924490b98d535486ec9da1c3850aee3e8f4574861e3c9.jpg)
  - values: (5.0, 20.0, 135.0)
  - In fact, the grade CGB graphite obtained 5 years ago for the MSRE satisfies this requirement.[20] However, the graphite structure must be much more restrictive to prevent gaseous fission products, particularly $^{135}\mathrm{Xe}$ , from diffusing into the graphite.
- new-developments-in-materials-for-molten-salt-reactors: NEW DEVELOPMENTS IN MATERIALS FOR MOLTEN-SALT REACTORS
  - values: (5.0, 21.0, 135.0)
  - In fact, the grade CGB graphite obtained 5 years ago for the MSRE satisfies this requirement.[21] However, the graphite structure must be much more restrictive to prevent gaseous fission products, particularly $^{135}\mathrm{Xe}$ , from diffusing into the graphite.

### measurement

- Template: `more recently the fused salt fluoride volatility process was used for removal of the <num> mathrm u - <num> mathrm u mixture from the msre fuel salt at the msre site after the reactor had operated for about <num> years. <num> this operation was also highly successful and the fuel carrier salt was subsequently combined with <num> mathrm u and returned to the msre for an additional year of operation.`
- Units: year, years
- Entities: reactor:MSRE
- ornl-5018: ![](images/96c48f13787c31854c9814da24c925e11be9013ba9fe9d693aa56a180614d0c1.jpg)
  - values: (235.0, 238.0, 1.5, 25.0, 233.0)
  - More recently the Fused Salt Fluoride Volatility Process was used for removal of the $^{235}\mathrm{U} - ^{238}\mathrm{U}$ mixture from the MSRE fuel salt at the MSRE site after the reactor had operated for about 1.5 years.[25] This operation was also highly successful, and the fuel carrier salt was subsequently combined with $^{233}\mathrm{U}$ and returned to the MSRE for an additional year of operation.
- ornl-tm-6415: ![](images/6f25af79db8446bcce7c7fe0081ce804cba28cc58dc153e91a50e4f262e223ad.jpg)
  - values: (235.0, 238.0, 1.5, 120.0, 233.0)
  - More recently the Fused Salt Fluoride Volatility Process was used for removal of the $^{235}\mathrm{U}-^{238}\mathrm{U}$ mixture from the MSRE fuel salt at the MSRE site after the reactor had operated for about 1.5 years.[120] This operation was also highly successful, and the fuel carrier salt was subsequently combined with $^{233}\mathrm{U}$ and returned to the MSRE for an additional year of operation.

### table-reference

- Template: `as shown by the results in tables <num> and <num> the ability to remove protactinium directly from the blanket stream has a marked effect on the fuel yield and lowers the fuel-cycle cost by about <num> mill/kwhr e .`
- Units: a
- ornl-3996: ORNL-3996
  - values: (1.0, 8.0, 0.1)
  - As shown by the results in Tables 1 and 8, the ability to remove protactinium directly from the blanket stream has a marked effect on the fuel yield and lowers the fuel-cycle cost by about 0.1 mill/kwhr(e).
- ornl-tm-1851: ORNL-TM-1851
  - values: (8.0, 15.0, 0.1)
  - As shown by the results in Tables 8 and 15 the ability to remove protactinium directly from the blanket stream has a marked effect on the fuel yield and lowers the fuel-cycle cost by about 0.1 mill/kwhr(e).

### table-reference

- Template: `earlier studies had demonstrated that mathrm uo <num> would not precipitate at <num> circ mathrm c from the solvent lif-bef <num> <num><num> mole % with added mathrm uf <num> and mathrm zrf <num> until the concentration ratio of mathrm zrf <num> to mathrm uf <num> dropped below about <num>. <num> therefore a preliminary study was made of the precipitation of mathrm pao <num> from a fluoride mixture known to have mathrm zro <num> as the stable oxide phase. <num>`
- Units: a, c
- Entities: salt-system:LiF
- ornl-3913: ORNL.3913
  - values: (2.0, 700.0, 2.0, 66.0, -34.0, 4.0, 4.0, 4.0, 4.0, 1.5, 15.0, 2.0, 2.0, 16.0)
  - Earlier studies had demonstrated that $\mathrm{UO}_2$ would not precipitate at $700^{\circ}\mathrm{C}$ from the solvent, LiF-BeF $_2$ (66-34 mole %) with added $\mathrm{UF_4}$ and $\mathrm{ZrF_4}$ , until the concentration ratio of $\mathrm{ZrF_4}$ to $\mathrm{UF_4}$ dropped below about 1.5.[15] Therefore, a preliminary study was made of the precipitation of $\mathrm{PaO}_2$ from a fluoride mixture known to have $\mathrm{ZrO}_2$ as the stable oxide phase.[16]
- ornl-3936: ORNL-3936
  - values: (2.0, 700.0, 2.0, 66.0, -34.0, 4.0, 4.0, 4.0, 4.0, 1.5, 22.0, 2.0, 2.0, 23.0)
  - Earlier studies had demonstrated that $\mathrm{UO_2}$ would not precipitate at $700^{\circ}\mathrm{C}$ from the solvent, LiF-BeF $_2$ (66-34 mole %) with added $\mathrm{UF_4}$ and $\mathrm{ZrF_4}$ , until the concentration ratio of $\mathrm{ZrF_4}$ to $\mathrm{UF_4}$ dropped below about 1.5.[22] Therefore a preliminary study was made of the precipitation of $\mathrm{PaO_2}$ from a fluoride mixture known to have $\mathrm{ZrO_2}$ as the stable oxide phase.[23]

- Template: `the data in table <num> show that <num> % of the protactinium was precipitated in a form that would not pass through a sintered copper filter after a fairly short exposure to solid thorium but nearly <num> % was in the unfiltered salt that was transferred back to the nickel vessel after exposure to thorium.`
- Units: a
- Entities: alloy-material:nickel
- ornl-4076: HeoHcTo 95hE
  - values: (3.1, 99.0, 7.0)
  - The data in Table 3.1 show that $99\%$ of the protactinium was precipitated in a form that would not pass through a sintered copper filter after a fairly short exposure to solid thorium, but nearly $7\%$ was in the unfiltered salt that was transferred back to the nickel vessel after exposure to thorium.
- ornl-tm-1853: OAK RIDGE NATIONAL LABORATORY
  - values: (15.0, 99.0, 7.0)
  - The data in Table 15 show that $99\%$ of the protactinium was precipitated in a form that would not pass through a sintered copper filter after a fairly short exposure to solid thorium, but nearly $7\%$ was in the unfiltered salt that was transferred back to the nickel vessel after exposure to thorium.
